Critical RCE flaw in xrdp remote desktop server allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code via crafted UTF-16 domain names that trigger stack buffer overflow.  Key technical details: • CVE-2025-68670 (CVSS not specified) affects xrdp versions prior to 0.10.5, 0.9.27, and 0.10.4.1 • Vulnerability in xrdp_wm_parse_domain_information() function processes 512-byte UTF-8 domain into 256-byte buffer • Exploitation occurs during pre-auth Secure Settings Exchange via Client Info PDU (T1210) • Attack vector: domain name starting with "_" followed by >256 UTF-8 bytes before "__" delimiter  Attack methodology: • Attacker crafts malicious .rdp file with oversized domain field using UTF-16 to UTF-8 conversion differences • Uses Cyrillic characters (U+041A) to maximize UTF-8 expansion while staying under 512-byte limit • Buffer overflow overwrites stack return address, enabling ROP chain execution • Stack canaries provide partial protection but can be bypassed with value leakage  DFIR artifacts: • Monitor for unusual .rdp file creation/modification with abnormally long domain fields • Check xrdp server logs for domain parsing errors or crashes during connection establishment • Examine network traffic for RDP Client Info PDUs with malformed domain strings  Patch immediately to fixed versions. Hunt for suspicious .rdp files and correlate xrdp crashes with connection attempts.  #DFIR_Radar

How easy is CVE-2026-23918 to trigger?  🔸 One TCP connection. 🔸 Two frames. 🔸 HEADERS + immediate RST_STREAM (non-zero error code).  That’s it → double-free in mod_http2, worker crashes.  Researchers built a working RCE PoC using Apache’s fixed scoreboard + mmap allocator (default on Debian & official Docker).  If you’re on 2.4.66 with mod_http2 + threaded MPM: patch to 2.4.67 now. (prefork MPM is safe)

【vm2 CVE-2026-26956、Node.js 25環境でホストコード実行の恐れ】  BleepingComputerは、Node.js向けサンドボックスライブラリvm2のCVE-2026-26956について、サンドボックス脱出によりホスト上の任意コード実行が可能になると報じています。影響が確認されたのはvm2 3.10.4とNode.js 25環境です。  この種の脆弱性は、オンラインコード実行、SaaS自動化、ワークフローエンジン、プラグイン実行基盤で特に危険です。ユーザーが投入したJavaScriptがサンドボックスを抜けると、環境変数、内部API、クラウドキー、CI/CDシークレットに到達する可能性があります。  防御側は、3.10.5以降、できれば最新の3.11.2以降へ更新してください。さらに、短命コンテナ、egress deny by default、rootless実行、秘密情報の分離を組み合わせ、vm2単体を最後の防壁にしない設計が必要です。  #vm2 #CVE202626956 #Nodejs #SandboxEscape #DevSecOps #RCE bleepingcomputer.com/news/se…

【PCPJack、クラウド資格情報を狙うワーム的スティーラーに注意】  The Hacker Newsは、PCPJackという新しい資格情報窃取フレームワークを報じています。PCPJackは公開クラウド基盤を標的にし、複数CVEを悪用しながら、クラウド資格情報を収集して拡散するワーム的な挙動を持つとされています。  クラウド環境では、1台の公開ホスト侵害が環境変数、IMDS、CI/CDシークレット、クラウドCLI設定、IAMキーへつながります。盗まれたキーは、正規API経由の横展開、ストレージ窃取、リソース不正作成、暗号資産マイニング、アクセス販売に転用され得ます。  防御側は、公開資産のCVE管理、IMDSv2強制、最小権限IAM、長期アクセスキーの削減、CI/CDシークレットの分離を優先してください。検知では、未知リージョンのクラウドAPI操作、短時間の大量リソース作成、メタデータアクセス、セキュリティグループ変更を確認します。  #PCPJack #CloudSecurity #CredentialTheft #IAM #DevSecOps #ThreatIntel thehackernews.com/2026/05/pc…

【PAN-OS RCE、4月中旬に成功か。EarthWorm/ReverseSocks5投入まで確認】  The Hacker Newsは、PAN-OS CVE-2026-0300の悪用について、攻撃者が4月9日頃から試行し、4月中旬に未認証RCEへ成功したと報じています。成功後はNginx workerへシェルコードを注入し、痕跡削除を行ったとされています。  さらに4月29日には、EarthWormやReverseSocks5といったプロキシ/トンネリング系ツールが投入されたとされます。これは、境界ファイアウォール侵害が内部ネットワーク偵察や長期アクセスに使われた可能性を示します。  防御側は、User-ID Authentication Portalの外部到達性を停止し、Response Pagesを無効化し、Threat ID 510019を確認してください。過去ログでは、Nginxクラッシュ、core dump削除、AD偵察、未知の外向きトンネル通信を重点的に見ます。  #PANOS #CVE20260300 #EarthWorm #ReverseSocks5 #Firewall #ThreatHunting thehackernews.com/2026/05/pa…

【PAN-OSゼロデイ、国家支援型を示唆するポストエクスプロイト活動】  SecurityWeekは、PAN-OS CVE-2026-0300の悪用について、中国国家支援型活動を想起させる痕跡があると報じています。Palo Alto Networks自体は国家名を断定していませんが、Unit 42はCL-STA-1132をlikely state-sponsoredとして追跡しています。  攻撃者は4月9日頃から試行し、約1週間後にRCEへ成功したとされます。成功後はNginx worker processへシェルコードを注入し、クラッシュログ、core dump、Nginx関連痕跡を削除して検知を避けようとしました。  境界ファイアウォールの侵害は、内部AD偵察、プロキシ投入、認証情報や証明書窃取へ直結します。防御側はパッチ待ちではなく、Portal制限、ログ欠損確認、AD通信、EarthWorm/ReverseSocks5類似通信を優先的に調査してください。  #PaloAlto #PANOS #CVE20260300 #CyberEspionage #APT #FirewallSecurity securityweek.com/palo-alto-z…

【Unit 42分析：PAN-OS CVE-2026-0300、Nginx workerへシェルコード注入】  Unit 42は、PAN-OS CVE-2026-0300の悪用について、CL-STA-1132として追跡するlikely state-sponsored活動を公表しました。攻撃者はUser-ID Authentication Portalの脆弱性を悪用し、未認証RCEに成功した後、Nginx worker processへシェルコードを注入したとされています。  この攻撃で重要なのは、侵害後にクラッシュログやcore dumpを削除し、検知を避けようとしている点です。さらに、Active Directory偵察やプロキシ系ツール投入に進んだとされ、単なる脆弱性スキャンではなく諜報型のポストエクスプロイト活動と見なすべきです。  防御側は、User-ID Authentication Portalの外部公開停止、Response Pages無効化、Threat ID 510019の監視に加え、Nginxクラッシュ痕跡、ログ欠損、AD偵察、EarthWorm/ReverseSocks5風通信を確認してください。  #PANOS #PaloAltoNetworks #CVE20260300 #Unit42 #ZeroDay #APT #SOC unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/…

【Ivanti EPMM、過去侵害資格情報がCVE-2026-6973悪用に関係か】  ベルギーCCBは、Ivanti EPMMのCVE-2026-6973について、即時パッチを呼びかけています。注目点は、今回の悪用に使われた管理者資格情報が、2026年1月に公表されたCVE-2026-1340の過去悪用で得られた可能性が高いとされている点です。  つまり、EPMMの問題は「今回のパッチを当てるか」だけでは終わりません。過去侵害後に管理者資格情報、APIトークン、証明書、連携情報をローテーションしていない環境では、別の脆弱性や正規ログインを使って再侵入される可能性があります。  防御側は、EPMMの更新、管理者アカウントの削減、全管理者資格情報のローテーション、過去90日以上のログ確認を実施してください。特にモバイル証明書、Sentry連携、デバイス登録情報へのアクセスを確認すべきです。  #Ivanti #EPMM #CVE20266973 #CredentialTheft #MDM #IncidentResponse ccb.belgium.be/advisories/wa…

【CISA KEV入り：Ivanti EPMM CVE-2026-6973は5月10日期限の緊急対応対象】  The Hacker Newsは、Ivanti EPMMのCVE-2026-6973が限定的に悪用され、CISA KEVへ追加されたと報じています。対象はオンプレミス版EPMMで、12.6.1.1、12.7.0.1、12.8.0.1より前のバージョンです。  この脆弱性は管理者認証が必要なRCEですが、過去侵害で管理者資格情報が残っている環境では危険度が大きく上がります。CISAは米連邦機関に5月10日までの対応を求めており、民間組織でも優先順位付けの根拠になります。  防御側はパッチ適用に加え、管理者アカウントの棚卸し、過去侵害時の資格情報ローテーション確認、EPMM管理操作ログ、証明書操作、Sentry連携、未知の管理者追加を確認してください。  #Ivanti #EPMM #CISA #KEV #CVE20266973 #RCE #SOC thehackernews.com/2026/05/iv…

#threatreport #LowCompleteness Lazarus Group Uses Git Hooks To Hide Malware | 06-05-2026 Source: opensourcemalware.com/blog/d… Key details below ↓  🧑‍💻Actors/Campaigns: Lazarus Contagious_interview  💀Threats: Invisibleferret, Beavertail, Supply_chain_technique,  🎯Victims: Software developers, Cryptocurrency sector, Decentralized finance sector  🌐Geo: Dprk  🤖LLM extracted TTPs:` T1036.008, T1059.004, T1071.001, T1082, T1105, T1204, T1546, T1656  🧨IOCs:  - File: 4  - Domain: 1  - Url: 3  - Path: 1  💽Software: curl, Linux  🔢Algorithms: sha256  🔠Functions: TaskJacker  #threatreport: The Lazarus Group, linked to North Korea, has introduced a new technique in their Contagious Interview and TaskJacker campaigns by embedding a second-stage loader within Git hooks. This marks a departure from their previous methods of concealing the loader in files such as .vscode/tasks.json, package.json postinstall scripts, or fake .woff2 font files. The new step occurs right after a candidate clones a coding assessment repository, wherein the malicious loader executes prior to the commitment of any code changes.  The malicious action takes place through a `.githooks/pre-commit` script, which acts as a lightweight loader responsible for identifying the operating system of the victim through the `uname -s` command. It subsequently downloads a platform-specific payload from the designated control server at precommit.vercel.app using `curl` or `wget`, piping the result directly into a shell on Unix-like systems or `cmd.exe` on Windows variants using Git Bash, MSYS, or Cygwin. Notably, the command sent to the server includes a `flag=5` query parameter, indicating a unique identifier for the campaign or variant, which reflects the group's consistent use of numeric flags for tracking their deliveries. The script is designed to suppress output by redirecting it to `/dev/null`, and it ensures a successful commit by always exiting with a status of 0, thereby avoiding any disruption the developer might notice.  This tactic continues to leverage social engineering under the guise of a fake recruitment process aimed at technical candidates. The primary goal remains the same: to deploy malware, specifically the InvisibleFerret-style implants for crypto wallet and credential theft. The actors have been observed committing this same pre-commit hook across multiple GitHub repositories, which showcase typical Contagious Interview patterns, including the project themes of decentralized finance or cryptocurrency, new account creations, and minimal commit histories that encourage candidates to execute code locally.  Additionally, the group is also utilizing post-checkout hooks, which activate upon branch changes, enhancing the stealth of their operations. The ongoing evolution of the loader's location highlights the increasing sophistication of the Lazarus Group, which is searching for ever more effective ways to implant malware during developers’ workflows. This strategic adaptation signals that as long as developers run arbitrary scripts automatically, the group will continue to embed malicious payloads, making detection and prevention a continuous challenge for cybersecurity efforts.

OTD: 3-0 V PNE (2023)   On this day in 2023 Sunderland secured the final Championship play-off spot after a 3-0 win away to Preston and some drama elsewhere. It was arguably the best away performance of that season as well as the best away end. Here's Sobs' match report from that day.  On an afternoon of high drama and ridiculously complicated possibilities, the Lads, my bonny Lads, produced a second half performance that was as good as you'll see anywhere. 3-0, other results went one way and another until it was all over and, incredibly, we'd made our second consecutive play-offs.  Anyway, having spent Sunday in a blindingly sunny Blackpool, it was sort of reassuring to get damp in Preston. One of their fans in the Market Tavern said if Blackburn were winning and our game was 0-0 he'd run on in last minute and score for us. Nice lad.  In our pale blue away kit, we faced away from our support and Joff started proceedings. Rory Delap's bairn had a great chance for PNE as they sort of got the upper hand after the opening few minutes, but he fired wide, and Patto had to do his job well to keep the home side out as they settled earlier and created a few chances. However, we were the side winning the corners and free kicks, and Neil curled a shot just wide before Joff, then Gooch, got on the end of good moves but missed the target when they should really have scored.  There were three added minutes in which the rain really came down, so there was a mad dash out the back for shelter on the whistle after a half in which we should have been at least two up. It might have been dry, but you could hardly breathe for tabs and vapes.  No Cirkin for the second half, with Pritch coming on, so Gooch went to left back as we went a) three or less at the back, and b) for broke. All of which basically meant all-out attack, and Preston simply couldn't cope with it. The opener looked inevitable when Ekwah burst into the box, but he fired into Woodman's face rather than dink it over him. Damn.  Ten minutes into a half in which the home side had barely got a kick, Amad got onto the loose ball when another nice passing move broke down and simply curled it up and over Woodman. Marvellous, just marvellous, and Pritch gestured to our fans "what's the other scores?" We couldn't help him.  To be honest, that just about fettled the Lilywhites, as you could see their heads go down. It was as wet as an otter's pocket out there, with spray coming up whenever the ball landed, but we were controlling things. We knocked it around at the back, they half-heartedly chased with no hope of getting there, and we patiently waited for openings to set Clarke or Roberts away down the sides.  Pritch then took a pass from the marauding Clarke on the edge, gave Woodman the eye, and fired into the other corner as their keeper stood stock still. Still half an hour to go, and it wasn't going to get any better for the home side.  Clarke telegraphed exactly what he was going to do, but they still let him do it and he duly stepped inside and curled it low into the far corner. A triumvirate of superb finishes, it really was. Then roars and cheers kept erupting as the ever-changing scores from Boro and Bermondsey kept coming in.  Gooch went off paggered, reducing our number of out-and-out defenders to one, replaced by Anderson, getting it back up to two. Gelhardt, Ekwah, and Amad made way for Lihadji, Ba, and Michut but we were basically passing the home side to death and they looked like they just wanted their beds. Another couple of goals would not have been unfair on them, and they must have been sick when four added minutes were announced.  The whistle went, the other results were already in but were at last confirmed, and we had at least another two games to add to an incredible season. The players naturally celebrated in front of us, with Luke singing along and Mogga holding his distance but displaying a grin wider than the world's proudest dad. As well he should, as he worked nothing short of a miracle with the division's youngest side, shorn of a centre forward, a captain in central midfield, and a string of central defenders.  We were applauded off by home fans and players alike, which was really nice. It was well worth getting wet in row two to see three quality finishes from a few yards, and it was even nicer to be up close and personal for the post-match nonsense. Top marks also to the stewards, who looked after things with a smile on their faces - and rain capes, the jammy buggers.  Man of the Match? No weak links at all, but for me Ekwah was outstanding - he grows in stature with every game and could (should) have a bonny career ahead of him. Hopefully on Wearside. He gets my nod.  Off we went to plodge back to the Guild pub and some outstanding craic with PNE lads -even if they did say I was getting a tad ahead of myself when I said our pale blue kit and passing style made us look like Man City.  For the record - 62% possession, and only one foul to their eighteen.  This team never cease to amaze, disappoint, frustrate, delight, surprise - ah, man, just think of more descriptive words, I've run out. This day just emphasised all that's good about being a Sunderland fan. Even when wet, drunded, as we say in Bish - now let's get back to Blackpool and give it some celebration.  ... and get drunded for a second time on the way back to the hotel. It's only watter.

군사재판 가동 개시:  오바마는 외국의 적으로 낙인찍히고, 워싱턴 D.C.에서는 군사재판이 시작되며, 비공개 명령은 딥 스테이트 소탕 작전의 불씨를 지핀다. 군법무관 부대(JAG)는 완전 배치 모드에 돌입했다. 이것이 마지막 단계다. 공화국이 반격에 나선다.  기밀 정보 유출 | 브리핑 코드: 아이언-이클립스/OB-적-001 "확인됨: 대상 [오바마, 버락 후세인]은 군사법규 88.12b항에 따라 외국 적국으로 지정될 기준을 충족합니다. 국내 보호 조치가 해제되었습니다. 자산 감시가 진행 중입니다. 재판소 승인이 부여되었습니다." – JAG REDFILE 823-A // 타임스탬프: 08/17/2025 // 내부 전용  관련 기사: 국가 안보 폭탄 발언: 오바마, 연방 형사 수사 대상 — 법무부는 미국 연방 형법 18조 241항을 적용한다 명령은 확정됐다. 목표는 정해졌다. 이제 실행에 옮길 시간이다. 도널드 J. 트럼프 대통령은 현대 미국 역사상 가장 중대한 군사 작전을 승인 했습니다 . 버락 오바마의 체포, 재판, 그리고 군사형 선고 - 그는 법적으로 외국 적 전투원으로 낙인찍혔다 .  이건 정치적인 술수가 아닙니다. 국가 안보를 위한 조치입니다. JAG 특수작전팀 이 현재 워싱턴 D.C.에서 활동하며 극비리에 재판소의 보안을 강화하고 있습니다.  내부 관계자 확인: 인질 구출팀 배치 완료. 관련 서류 최종 정리 완료. 재판 절차 진행 중.  유출된 문서 발췌본: 다크 베일 작전 "오바마의 국내 정치적 이미지는 언론 순응과 행정적 고립을 통해 유지되었습니다. 그러나 정보공개법 우회 승인에 따라 공개된 여러 건의 NSA 도청 및 신호정보(SIGINT) 자료는 [삭제됨]을 포함한 외국 기관과의 비밀 통신을 확인시켜 줍니다."  해당 인물은 국경, 군사, 정보 및 경제 기반 시설의 전략적 해체를 통해 세계주의자들이 미국의 주권 영역에 침투하는 것을 용이하게 했습니다. 권장 조치: 군사재판소 5단계 | 상태: 적극적 개입* – 레드파일: OP-DARKVEIL // 작성: 인텔 그룹 에코-5  amg-news.com/operation-redfi…

“우리도 미국에 정보를 제한하는 상응 조치를 하자"는 주장이 한국정부에서 나왔다 (경향신문. 2026.4.21). 기사에는 '정부 고위 관계자'라고 하는데 한 명이 아니다.   - 첫 번째 정부 관계자: “보안조사에서 유출 정황 발견 못했다” + “정보 제한 규모가 크지 않고 보완 중”이라는 발언.  - 또 다른 정부 관계자: “북한 관련 정보는 우리가 미국에게 제공하는 부분도 있다... 상응하는 조치를 검토할 수도 있다”고 직접 언급한 인물  이재명측 고위층(청와대 또는 관계부처) 또는 2명의 정부 관계자라는 추측만 있을 뿐 제대로된 소속과 이름도 밝히지 않았다.   반미 정부에서 어떤 종류의 '보안조사'를 했는지 알 수 없지만, 미국과 양방향으로 크로스 체크가 되야 결론을 내는것이지, 지들끼리 '정보 유출 없다'로 결론 내리는건 아무런 의미가 없다.  정동영의 군사 기밀 누설 여파로 미국은 한 달째 IMINT(위성 이미지 정보(첩보) 등을 한국에 제공하지 않고 있다. 한국 위성이 수적으로 증가했다고는 하지만, 경험을 토대로 한 분석기술은 미국이 앞서는게 현실이다.   미국의 ‘초고속·초정밀 경보 및 정찰’이 한반도 감시망을 완성하는 ‘천조국 위력’이고, 한·미 정보 협력이 대북 대비의 핵심이라는 현실을 다 알고있는데, 기밀을 유출한 그 사람만 모르는듯 싶다.   상응 조치로 미국과의 정보 공유를 끊는다는 발상 자체가  국가안보 의식이 결여된 종북적인 좌편향된 반국가세력임을 스스로 입증한 셈이다.

I watched a junior DFIR analyst almost accuse somebody wrongly because of timestamps.  He found a ZIP archive on a workstation and immediately concluded the user downloaded the files that same morning because the folder showed a fresh creation time.  The problem was he never checked the original metadata properly. He was looking only at what Windows displayed inside File Explorer.  I asked him whether he verified the filesystem timestamps, timezone settings, or whether the files were copied from another source.  The guy was confident until we started checking deeper.  Turns out the archive had been extracted that morning, but the actual files inside were much older. The suspect did not create the documents recently at all. The timestamps changed during extraction and movement between systems.  Small mistakes like this are dangerous in forensics because once you build the wrong timeline, your entire investigation can start going in the wrong direction.

For my Brazilian ThreaHunting/DFIR friends:  Been reversing a malware called “#CNABHunter” (NUikita), and this thing is way more interesting than a regular banking trojan.  At first I had to figure out what “CNAB240/400” even was — apparently it’s a financial file standard heavily used by Brazilian ERP/banking integrations.  The malware hunts for those files in environments running TOTVS, SAP, RM, Senior, Sankhya, etc., extracts transaction data, and waits for remote commands to modify payments.  Most interesting part: it doesn’t do dumb string replacement.  The malware appears to rebuild the entire financial record using the correct field positions to keep the file structurally valid for banking processing.  Maybe my interpretation of this behavior is wrong, but that’s what I’ve understood so far from reversing it.  C2: 104.245.245[.]50:5000

sample_tweets.text: Hantavirus Incident on Cruise Vessel Sparks Global Response, WHO Steps In to Clarify  So apparently the world has briefly entered another health headline season, but this time with a firm disclaimer attached. The World Health Organization has stepped in to clarify that a hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship is not the opening scene of another global pandemic sequel.  The situation unfolded aboard a Dutch-flagged vessel, the MV Hondius, somewhere between Argentina and Cabo Verde, where several passengers reportedly fell ill and multiple deaths were recorded. Naturally, global attention did what global attention does best, it sprinted straight toward the word outbreak.  But according to WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, everyone can relax slightly. There are about eight identified cases so far, a mix of confirmed and suspected infections, and while that number is tragic for those involved, it is still firmly placed in the category of contained incidents rather than global emergencies with dramatic theme music.  The illness in question, hantavirus, is not new to science. It typically involves transmission from rodents, which is already nature’s way of reminding humans that we do not actually own this planet, we are just visiting.

🚨🇪🇺🇨🇳$431 Billion Blunder: Why Europe’s China Tech Ban Looks Like a Costly Mistake  Europe is walking into another expensive trap with its eyes wide open. A new report warns that the EU’s plan to ban Chinese suppliers from 18 key sectors under its proposed Cybersecurity Act could cost a staggering $431 billion over the next five years.  🔸The Total Cost Breakdown  The China Chamber of Commerce to the EU (CCCEU) and KPMG calculate that ripping out reliable Chinese equipment would cost around $171 billion just for the hardware swaps. Add supply chain chaos, service breakdowns, job shifts, and legal battles, and the bill climbs fast.  Annual losses start at $46 billion in 2026, spike to $109 billion in 2028, and hover near $105 billion through 2030. These are not small numbers for an economy already struggling.  🔸Extra Hidden Costs  Beyond the equipment, another $120 billion in “social losses” await. A massive $104 billion of that comes from delaying digital upgrades and green energy projects — exactly what Europe claims it urgently needs. Even $3.9 billion in unemployment aid carries heavy weight, showing real pain for workers.  🔸Hardest-Hit Sectors and Countries  Logistics and manufacturing get slammed hardest at $134 billion, followed by energy ($94 billion) and telecom ($67 billion). Germany alone will shoulder almost half — $200 billion (46.4%) — thanks to its deep industrial ties with efficient Chinese tech. France will suffer in healthcare and public services, Spain in renewables. Sixteen smaller countries get off lighter, but the whole bloc still pays.  🔸Why the Numbers Matter  These figures dwarf the European Commission’s rosy estimates, which only counted $11–15 billion for 5G. Brussels stands accused of ignoring the full damage across countries, sectors, and long-term ripple effects. The Commission talks about slow, careful rollout, but skeptics see a policy driven more by politics than smart economics.  In the end, this feels like a repeat of Europe’s painful Russian energy mistake. After relying on cheap Russian gas for years, the EU followed U.S. pressure, cut ties, and suffered sky-high energy prices, inflation, and weakened industry.  Now, by targeting affordable and widely used Chinese tech — again seemingly to align with Washington — Europe risks another self-inflicted wound. Smarter cooperation with Chinese suppliers would clearly serve Europe’s interests far better than this expensive decoupling.  NewRulesGeo❗

A threat actor is advertising alleged access to government and law enforcement email accounts and police portals that could be abused for fraudulent Emergency Data Requests (EDRs), forged subpoenas, and account data acquisition.  The post claims the actor can facilitate unauthorized requests targeting major platforms including:  Instagram Facebook WhatsApp TikTok Snapchat Microsoft Apple  According to the listing, the actor is offering:  Alleged government or police email access Forged legal request documentation Domain abuse services Social media account data extraction services  This type of activity represents a significant threat because fraudulent legal requests and compromised law enforcement portals can be abused to obtain:  IP logs Device information Linked phone numbers and emails Private account metadata Potential message-related information  The post also references multiple countries’ alleged government or police domains, though none of the claims have been independently verified.  This activity aligns with a growing trend involving:  Social engineering against platform trust & safety teams Abuse of emergency disclosure request workflows Compromised government infrastructure Insider threat and identity impersonation operations  Organizations handling lawful access and emergency disclosure workflows should:  Strictly validate requester identities Monitor for anomalous requests Enforce hardware-backed MFA Review domain trust mechanisms Implement secondary verification procedures for emergency requests  Daily Dark Web is monitoring the activity for further indicators and potential victim confirmation.  #DDW #CyberSecurity #EDR #LawEnforcement #SocialEngineering #ThreatIntelligence #DarkWeb #Infosec

I can assert with a high degree of confidence ShinyHunters did not exfiltrate highly sensitive information.  Based on information I've received the primary information stolen from the schools is student names and email addresses. Furthermore, this has been confirmed by various media outlets.  This in of itself isn't bad.  The primary issue with this however is that it would expose children in K-12 online (first and last name). Adults having their full legal name and email address online is something you could (probably) find on LinkedIn or a university directory. Adults will be ignored if data is leaked. K-12 will be a nightmare. Hence, educational institutions must put together a strategy to handle a K-12 potential data leak.  Presumably parents will be outraged and this will inevitably result in a lawsuit against the schools or Canvas.  The much larger issue however is the catastrophic damage ShinyHunters has done to Canvas both operational and reputational.  Exfiltrating data from a compromised host is as simple as initializing a file transfer. The question then: why is Canvas still "in maintenance mode"? The only logical conclusion is ShinyHunters did SOMETHING to prevent Canvas from working as intended.  This places Canvas is a terrible, terrible, terrible position. Their service has resulted in minors having their names (potentially) leaked and educational institutions can't use the platform they pay for. Furthermore, this makes major educational institutions look like a bunch of morons.  Students are paying top dollar for an education and suddenly ... poof ... a good chunk of their work or study material has vaporized because it was stored in a 3rd party platform outside the control of the educational institution.  Basically, the data breach itself isn't bad except the K-12 part. The operational impact is devastating and the fallout will be a nightmare. Canvas employees are probably scrambling, their cybersecurity team is probably having panic attacks, and executive leadership is probably drunk right now screaming at the wall.

🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-05-08 18:21 KST
🟠 위협 점수: 8/10 (높음)

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 사이버 위협은 Linux 커널 취약점(Dirty Frag)을 이용한 LPE(Local Privilege Escalation)와 클라우드 인프라를 타겟으로 하는 자동화된 크리덴셜 탈취 프레임워크(PCPJack)의 확산이 두드러집니다. 특히 교육 플랫폼(Canvas)을 대상으로 한 대규모 데이터 갈취 캠페인과 같이 공급망 및 SaaS 환경을 겨냥한 공격이 빈번해지고 있습니다. 또한, AI 모델을 활용한 취약점 탐색 및 공격 기법이 고도화됨에 따라 기업의 보안 운영 환경 전반에 걸친 선제적 대응이 요구되는 시점입니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
*   **ShinyHunters**: 교육 플랫폼 Canvas LMS를 대상으로 대규모 데이터 갈취 및 서비스 마비 공격 수행.
*   **PCPJack**: 클라우드 환경(AWS, Docker, K8s)을 타겟으로 TeamPCP 아티팩트를 제거하고 크리덴셜을 탈취하는 웜 형태의 악성코드 프레임워크.
*   **CL-STA-1132 (Unit 42)**: PAN-OS CVE-2026-0300 취약점을 악용하여 Nginx worker에 쉘코드를 주입하는 국가 배후(likely state-sponsored) 공격 그룹.
*   **ScarCruft**: Windows 및 Android 게임 플랫폼을 타겟으로 백도어를 유포하는 공급망 공격 수행.

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
*   **국가정보원법 개정안**: 국가 배후 해킹 의심 사례에 대한 민간 조사 권한 확대 논란이 제기됨.
*   **안랩(AhnLab)**: 대만 CYBERSEC 2026에서 엔드포인트 및 CPS 보안 플랫폼을 선보이며 중화권 시장 공략 강화.
*   **기타**: 이번 수집 데이터 내 한국 기업/기관을 직접 겨냥한 구체적인 침해 사고는 보고되지 않았으나, 글로벌 공급망 공격의 영향권에 노출될 가능성이 상존함.

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
*   **CVE-2026-6973**: Ivanti EPMM, CVSS 7.2, Active Exploitation(CISA KEV 등재).
*   **CVE-2026-31431**: Linux Kernel (Dirty Frag), LPE, Active Exploitation.
*   **CVE-2026-0300**: PAN-OS User-ID Authentication Portal, RCE, Active Exploitation.
*   **CVE-2025-68670**: xrdp server, RCE, Pre-auth.
*   **CVE-2026-26956**: vm2 sandbox, RCE(Node.js 25 환경).

### 5. IOC 요약
*   **CVE**:
    *   CVE-2026-31431 ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/05/linux-kernel-dirty-frag-lpe-exploit.html), [SANS ISC](https://isc.sans.edu/diary/rss/32968), [Unit42](https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/cve-2026-31431-copy-fail/))
    *   CVE-2026-6973 ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/05/ivanti-epmm-cve-2026-6973-rce-under.html))
    *   CVE-2026-0300 ([Unit42](https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/captive-portal-zero-day/))
    *   CVE-2025-68670 ([Securelist](https://securelist.com/cve-2025-68670/119742/))

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
*   **Ivanti EPMM 패치 적용**: CVE-2026-6973에 대한 최신 보안 업데이트를 즉시 적용하고, 관리자 계정의 크리덴셜을 재설정하십시오.
*   **Linux 커널 보안 점검**: Dirty Frag(CVE-2026-31431) 취약점 대응을 위해 배포판별 최신 커널 패치를 확인하고, 불필요한 권한 상승 경로를 차단하십시오.
*   **클라우드 환경 모니터링**: PCPJack과 같은 클라우드 타겟 악성코드 탐지를 위해 CI/CD 파이프라인 및 클라우드 환경 변수 접근 로그를 정밀 모니터링하십시오.

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
*   **점수**: 8/10
*   **근거**: Linux 커널 제로데이와 Ivanti EPMM 등 주요 인프라 취약점이 실시간으로 악용되고 있으며, 클라우드 환경을 노리는 자동화된 공격이 확산되고 있어 매우 위험함.
